description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages, test and release, using a global docker:dind service (with --experimental flag) and variables BUILDX_PLATFORM, BUILDX_URL, DOCKER_BUILDKIT, DOCKER_CLI_EXPERIMENTAL, DOCKER_DRIVER, DOCKER_HOST, and GO_VERSION. In the test stage add a dependency-scan job that uses the snyk/snyk:golang-1.23 image (cleared entrypoint), runs `snyk auth $SNYK_TOKEN` then `snyk test --file=go.mod`, allows failure and uses the docker:dind service; an image-scan job that uses snyk/snyk-cli:1.1228.0-docker, runs `touch vaultbot`, `snyk auth $SNYK_TOKEN`, `docker build -t msvechla/vaultbot:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} .` and `snyk test --docker msvechla/vaultbot:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} --file=Dockerfile`, also allowed to fail; a lint job with golangci/golangci-lint:v1.59.1-alpine that runs `golangci-lint run --issues-exit-code 0 --exclude 'Using the variable on range scope `tt` in function literal' --out-format code-climate | tee gl-code-quality-report.json | jq -r '.[] | "\(.location.path):\(.location.lines.begin) \(.description)"'` and stores gl-code-quality-report.json as a code‑quality artifact; and a test job (default docker:latest image) that runs `sh testdata/run.sh` and a docker run command executing Go tests with coverage, publishing the testoutput directory as an artifact on failure. In the release stage add a release job using docker:26 that runs before_script steps to install Docker Buildx, enable experimental mode, load QEMU, create a multi‑arch builder, write the GPG key, then executes a privileged docker run invoking `goreleaser/goreleaser:v2.6.1 release --clean`, runs only on tag pipelines, and defines Docker registry variables; also add a semantic-release job using node:21 that runs `npm install` then `npx semantic-release` and runs only on the master branch.
